content:
To my knowledge, there is no manual. My suggestion is to download from thunderbird.net and install, set up an email account, and then do some browsing of the various menus. You will find you learn thunderbird best by touching it. Here are two links that may help:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/getting-started-thunderbird-main-window-supernova

https://blog.thunderbird.net/2023/08/make-thunderbird-yours-how-to-get-the-thunderbird-115-supernova-look/
creator: davidsk

Yeah, mailwasher accesses the email using POP and the default action of Google is to only offer email once over POP.

Go here https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#settings/fwdandpop Correct your settings on Gmail for your use case.

content:
Did you do what I said ? No you did not. I said

Go here https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#settings/fwdandpop

You did something else entirely changing things in Thunderbird Your issue has nothing at all to do with Thunderbird the issue lies in your setting on the google web site. I would guess you need the "keep gmails copy in the inbox setting" but I really do not know for sure because I simply do not use mailwasher. This issue is something caused by the interaction of mailwasher with google. You need to fix that not Thunderbird even though your perception is Thunderbird is not working.

What you are doing here I have no idea by you need to change it back post hast. That setting simply will not work at all the account is configured in Thunderbird as IMAP and attempting to connect using IMAP will result in a connection failure. YEs it says it connects, but it will not work as soon as the conversation goes to actually trying to get mail instead of just connecting.

The correct server name for a GMail IMAP mail account is imap.gmail.com on port 993.

content: Thunderbird is a free, open-source program and it costs nothing to download, install, or use. When you download it from the official web site https://www.thunderbird.net/ you will be presented with a request to donate, but it is not required and the installer will continue to download. The request screen can be ignored or dismissed by clicking the X in the upper right corner, as you can see in the attached image.
creator: rednoise

content:
See also https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/thunderbird-faq#w_is-thunderbird-free

Is Thunderbird free? Yes! Thunderbird is open source software. Anyone can download and use the program for free, and view and modify the source code under the terms of the license.

The asking for donation is optional and is not a requirement in order to download.

Check the downloads list in your Firefox web browser as you may have downloaded the Thunderbird setup in background.
creator: James
